preamble: The shift from 'pʲ' to 'c' (voiceless palatal stop) represents a rare but phonetically documented shift in place of articulation (fronting/shift). The palatal secondary articulation of the bilabial stop becomes the primary articulation.

examples.note: Rare dialectal shifts where palatalized bilabials are reanalyzed as palatal stops.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
